atoms.source_snippet:
RPC-level validation exists to provide immediate user feedback... This is a UX improvement, not consensus enforcement.

pub(crate) fn validate_async_payload(precompile_address: Address, input: &Bytes) -> Result<(), AsyncValidationError> { validation::validate_async_payload(precompile_address, input).map_err(Into::into) }
atoms.semantic_claim: The old RPC layer validates async precompile payload ABI, TTL, executor zero-address, URL/model/message shape, and input size before accepting async transactions into the pool.

destinations.evidence: Static payload validation and focused tests exist in `ritual-precompile-codecs`; no `reth-rpc` RPC preflight wrapper exists.
Builder-side async payload validation calls the shared validator.
Verifier-side async payload validation calls the shared validator.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Non-exact: the consensus-relevant static validation exists in shared builder/verifier paths, but the old immediate RPC rejection/user-feedback wrapper is not present in `reth-rpc`.
atoms.test_strategy: shared validator unit tests plus RPC sendRawTransaction negative test
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit an async HTTP precompile transaction with TTL 0 through RPC and verify whether the destination rejects immediately or only later skips/rejects in builder/verifier paths.
atoms.notes: Definitive after current-edits check: core builder/verifier/shared-codec validation remains covered, but old immediate RPC preflight/user-feedback behavior is not restored.

atoms.source_snippet: validate_executor_registration(state, executor, precompile_address) queries the TEE registry and checks required Capability for HTTP, LLM, sovereign/persistent agent, media, ZK, and FHE precompiles.
atoms.semantic_claim: The old RPC preflight checks executor registration and maps each async precompile to an allowed TEE capability before pool admission completes.

destinations.evidence: Builder path reads executor registry and validates async capability.
Verifier path reads executor registry for async/SPC validation.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Non-exact: executor/capability checks are present in live builder/verifier validation, but the old RPC-level pre-admission capability error path is absent.
atoms.test_strategy: builder/verifier capability regression plus RPC preflight check
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Use an executor registered with the wrong capability and compare destination RPC response timing with old immediate `ExecutorWrongCapability` behavior.
atoms.notes: Definitive after current-edits check: core builder/verifier/shared-codec validation remains covered, but old immediate RPC preflight/user-feedback behavior is not restored.

atoms.source_snippet: validate_llm_model_registration reads ModelRegistry; validate_wallet_for_async delegates to verify_wallet_for_commitment and translates insufficient balance, lock duration, and model errors into RPC-facing errors.
atoms.semantic_claim: The old RPC preflight validates LLM model registration and wallet balance/lock duration using the same shared commitment verifier as the builder.

destinations.evidence: Builder path uses shared fee/model validation.
Verifier path uses shared wallet validation.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Non-exact: wallet/model validity remains enforced in live async execution/verification, but the old RPC-specific descriptive preflight errors are not exact-port preserved.
atoms.test_strategy: wallet/model negative tests at RPC and builder/verifier boundaries
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit an async LLM transaction with an unregistered model or insufficient wallet lock and verify whether RPC returns the old descriptive error before pool admission.
atoms.notes: Definitive after current-edits check: core builder/verifier/shared-codec validation remains covered, but old immediate RPC preflight/user-feedback behavior is not restored.

atoms.source_snippet: validate_two_phase_deadline delegates offset-vs-TTL checks; tests cover valid HTTP request, ttl_zero, ttl_exceeded, zero_executor, empty_url, invalid_url_scheme, malformed_abi, and input_too_large.
atoms.semantic_claim: The old RPC preflight rejects invalid two-phase deadline offsets and carries unit coverage for common async payload negative cases.

destinations.evidence: Shared codec validation includes payload/deadline validation and tests for the static negative cases.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Non-exact: validation logic and static tests are preserved in the shared codec crate, but the old RPC-local test module and preflight deadline rejection surface are absent.
atoms.test_strategy: shared validation unit tests plus RPC two-phase send negative test
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit a long-running/two-phase precompile with deadline offset <= TTL and check whether destination RPC rejects immediately.
atoms.notes: Definitive after current-edits check: core builder/verifier/shared-codec validation remains covered, but old immediate RPC preflight/user-feedback behavior is not restored.

summary: Confirmed non-exact RPC/API behavior: old RPC async preflight validation module remains absent. Core async validation is still enforced by shared codec, payload builder, and verifier paths, so this is not a final-chain FSM gap.
notes: Current working tree has no `crates/rpc/rpc/src/async_tx_validator.rs` implementation and no `simulate_for_rpc_validation` use in `crates/rpc/rpc/src`.
Static payload/deadline validation remains in `ritual-precompile-codecs`; executor/wallet/model validation remains in payload builder and block verification.
This is confirmed non-parity for immediate RPC user feedback/pre-admission validation, not for consensus/payload validation authority.
